c:\windows\system32\windowspowershell\v1.0\powershell.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-12288   creator process id: 0x1308   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\cmd.exe   process command line: powershell  -noninteractive -windowstyle hidden -command "& {mode con lines=1 cols=32766; set-variable -name 'snc_iswmi' -value $true -scope global;  function printvalues{process{foreach-object{try{$a=$_.properties}catch{$a=$null}if(-not $a){$a=$_.psobject.properties}foreach($p in $a){$o=$p.name+' : ';if ($p.value -is [array]){$o+=[string]::join(',', $p.value);}else{$o+=$p.value;}$o;if($p.value -is [system.management.managementbaseobject] -or $p.value -is [system.management.managementbaseobject[]]){'_extended_start:';$p.value|printvalues;'_extended_end!';}}}}}gwmi -namespace root\cimv2 win32_operatingsystem -ea silentlycontinue|select __class,caption,version|printvalues|format-list;'---snc-seperator---registry-section---';get-itemproperty -path 'registry::hkey_users/*/software/adobe/adobe acrobat/dc/aventitlement' -ea pspath,installdate,displayname,displayversion,parentdisplayname,publisher,uninstallstring,installlocation|format-list;get-itemproperty -path 'hklm:/software/wow6432node/microsoft/office/*/registration/*' -ea silentlycontinue|select pspath,digitalproductid,productid|format-list;get-itemproperty -path 'registry::hkey_users/*/software/microsoft/windows/currentversion/uninstall/*' -ea silentlycontinue|select pspath,uninstallstring,displayname,displayversion,publisher,parentdisplayname|format-list;get-itemproperty -path 'hklm:/software/microsoft/windows/currentversion/installer/userdata/*/products/*/installproperties' -ea silentlycontinue|select pspath,installdate,installlocation,publisher,productid,displayname,displayversion|format-list;get-itemproperty -path 'hklm:/software/wow6432node/microsoft/inetstp' -ea silentlycontinue|select pspath,versionstring,setupstring|format-list;}"     token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for a powershell command with a very long argument. malware delivered via powershell will often execute a command with an entire script as its argument. in many cases, these commands are encoded and/or obfuscated. this may trigger an alert on legitimate powershell activity. in those cases, the commands will not be encoded or obfuscated.
search: ((metaclass:windows ((class=ms_defender category:`advancedhunting-deviceprocessevents`) or (source:`microsoft-windows-sysmon` eventid=1) or (source:`microsoft-windows-security-auditing` eventid=4688))) or (class:fireeye_hx_ioc assets.properties.os:/windows/ eventtype:processevent)) [process,processpath]:`\powershell.exe` length(args)>2000 not args:`token elevation type indicates*` not srcipv4:$exclusions.global.srcipv4
tuning_search: not (username=zenmonitor args:`management.automation.host.size` args:[`get-counter`,`import-module failoverclusters`,`hkcu:\software\zenoss\logs`,`microsoft.sqlserver.management.smo.table`,`microsoft.sqlserver.management.smo.agent.job`]) not (username=[ansibleadmin,peoplesoft_patching] args:/[a-za-z0-9\/+]{500,}/) not (username=tenable args:`num_jars_found`)

description: this rule looks for a command with a very long argument. malware authors and windows attack tools often perform a variety of tasks using built-in commands. commands with a long list of arguments could indicate malicious activity. legitimate commands triggered by this rule should be tuned accordingly.

description: this rule looks for a powershell command with a very long argument. malware delivered via powershell will often execute a command with an entire script as its argument. in many cases, these commands are encoded and/or obfuscated. this may trigger an alert on legitimate powershell activity. in those cases, the commands will not be encoded or obfuscated.

pspath,versionstring,setupstring|format-list;}"     token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: powershell is a scripting language and command-line shell that is bundled with all modern versions of windows. while this tool is used for all kinds of legitimate purposes, attackers also leverage it to compromise windows systems. additionally, attackers frequently use obfuscation techniques in an attempt to bypass detection mechanisms. however, if obfuscation is detected, it can be a telltale sign of malicious intent. this rule detects a powershell argument that has high number of dollar sign characters, indicating many variables were set. this is likely an unusual thing to see on the command line and could be an indicator of string obfuscation.

- Thad","closed_state":"Unknown","confidence":"Medium","context":null,"description":"PowerShell is a scripting language and command-line shell that is bundled with all modern versions of Windows. While this tool is used for all kinds of legitimate purposes, attackers also leverage it to compromise Windows systems. Additionally, attackers frequently use obfuscation techniques in an attempt to bypass detection mechanisms. However, if obfuscation is detected, it can be a telltale sign of malicious intent. This rule detects a PowerShell argument that has high number of dollar sign characters, indicating many variables were set. description: this rule looks for a powershell command with a very long argument. malware delivered via powershell will often execute a command with an entire script as its argument. in many cases, these commands are encoded and/or obfuscated. this may trigger an alert on legitimate powershell activity. in those cases, the commands will not be encoded or obfuscated.
search: ((metaclass:windows ((class=ms_defender category:`advancedhunting-deviceprocessevents`) or (source:`microsoft-windows-sysmon` eventid=1) or (source:`microsoft-windows-security-auditing` eventid=4688))) or (class:fireeye_hx_ioc assets.properties.os:/windows/ eventtype:processevent)) [process,processpath]:`\powershell.exe` length(args)>2000 not args:`token elevation type indicates*` not srcipv4:$exclusions.global.srcipv4
tuning_search: not (username=zenmonitor args:`management.automation.host.size` args:[`get-counter`,`import-module failoverclusters`,`hkcu:\software\zenoss\logs`,`microsoft.sqlserver.management.smo.table`,`microsoft.sqlserver.management.smo.agent.job`]) not (username=[ansibleadmin,peoplesoft_patching] args:/[a-za-z0-9\/+]{500,}/) not (username=tenable args:`num_jars_found`)

pspath,versionstring,setupstring|format-list;}"     token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for a powershell command with a very long argument. malware delivered via powershell will often execute a command with an entire script as its argument. in many cases, these commands are encoded and/or obfuscated. this may trigger an alert on legitimate powershell activity. in those cases, the commands will not be encoded or obfuscated.
search: ((metaclass:windows ((class=ms_defender category:`advancedhunting-deviceprocessevents`) or (source:`microsoft-windows-sysmon` eventid=1) or (source:`microsoft-windows-security-auditing` eventid=4688))) or (class:fireeye_hx_ioc assets.properties.os:/windows/ eventtype:processevent)) [process,processpath]:`\powershell.exe` length(args)>2000 not args:`token elevation type indicates*` not srcipv4:$exclusions.global.srcipv4
tuning_search: not (username=zenmonitor args:`management.automation.host.size` args:[`get-counter`,`import-module failoverclusters`,`hkcu:\software\zenoss\logs`,`microsoft.sqlserver.management.smo.table`,`microsoft.sqlserver.management.smo.agent.job`]) not (username=[ansibleadmin,peoplesoft_patching] args:/[a-za-z0-9\/+]{500,}/) not (username=tenable args:`num_jars_found`)

the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: powershell is a scripting language and command-line shell that is bundled with all modern versions of windows. while this tool is used for all kinds of legitimate purposes, attackers also leverage it to compromise windows systems. additionally, attackers frequently use obfuscation techniques in an attempt to bypass detection mechanisms. however, if obfuscation is detected, it can be a telltale sign of malicious intent. this rule detects a powershell argument that has high number of dollar sign characters, indicating many variables were set. this is likely an unusual thing to see on the command line and could be an indicator of string obfuscation.

This is likely an unusual thing to see on the command line and could be an indicator of string obfuscation.","distinguisher_key":"whpp004.parkview.com","distinguishers":{"hostname":"whpp004.parkview.com"},"events_count":2,"events_threshold":1,"external_id":"","first_event_at":"2025-07-23T11:13:48.290000Z","last_event_at":"2025-07-23T11:15:16.206000Z","external_ips":[],"external_ips_count":0,"info_links":["https:\/\/github.com\/danielbohannon\/Invoke-Obfuscation","https:\/\/www.youtube.com\/watch?v=P1lkflnWb0I","https:\/\/attack.mitre.org\/techniques\/T1027\/","https:\/\/attack.mitre.org\/techniques\/T1059\/001\/"],"internal_ips":[],"internal_ips_count":0,"is_suppressed":false,"is_threat":false,"is_tuned":true,"kill_chain":["5 - 'hklm:/system/currentcontrolset/services/tcpip/parameters' -ea silentlycontinue|select pspath,domain,hostname|format-list;}"     token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for a command with a very long argument. malware authors and windows attack tools often perform a variety of tasks using built-in commands. commands with a long list of arguments could indicate malicious activity. legitimate commands triggered by this rule should be tuned accordingly.

the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for a command with a very long argument. malware authors and windows attack tools often perform a variety of tasks using built-in commands. commands with a long list of arguments could indicate malicious activity. legitimate commands triggered by this rule should be tuned accordingly.

description: powershell is a scripting language and command-line shell that is bundled with all modern versions of windows. while this tool is used for all kinds of legitimate purposes, attackers also leverage it to compromise windows systems. additionally, attackers frequently use obfuscation techniques in an attempt to bypass detection mechanisms. however, if obfuscation is detected, it can be a telltale sign of malicious intent. this rule detects a powershell argument that has high number of dollar sign characters, indicating many variables were set. this is likely an unusual thing to see on the command line and could be an indicator of string obfuscation.

description: this rule looks for a command with a very long argument. malware authors and windows attack tools often perform a variety of tasks using built-in commands. commands with a long list of arguments could indicate malicious activity. legitimate commands triggered by this rule should be tuned accordingly.

 function printvalues{process{foreach-object{try{$a=$_.properties}catch{$a=$null}if(-not $a){$a=$_.psobject.properties}foreach($p in $a){$o=$p.name+' : ';if ($p.value -is mscluster_node -ea silentlycontinue|select __class,name|printvalues|format-list;'---snc-seperator---registry-section---';get-itemproperty -path 'hklm:/system/currentcontrolset/services/tcpip/parameters' -ea silentlycontinue|select pspath,domain,hostname|format-list;}"     token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.
detail.args: powershell  -NonInteractive -WindowStyle Hidden -command "& {mode con lines=1 cols=32766; Set-Variable -Name 'SNC_isWmi' -Value $true -Scope Global;  function printValues{Process{foreach-object{try{$a=$_.Properties}catch{$a=$null}if(-not $a){$a=$_.PsObject.Properties}foreach($p in $a){$o=$p.Name+' : ';if ($p.Value -is [Array]){$o+=[string]::Join(',', $p.Value);}else{$o+=$p.Value;}$o;if($p.Value -is [System.Management.ManagementBaseObject] -or $p.Value -is [System.Management.ManagementBaseObject[]]){'_EXTENDED_START:';$p.Value|printValues;'_EXTENDED_END!';}}}}}gwmi -namespace root/MSCluster MSCluster_Cluster -EA SilentlyContinue|select __CLASS,Name|printValues|format-list;gwmi -namespace root/MSCluster MSCluster_ClusterToResource -EA SilentlyContinue|select __CLASS,GroupComponent,PartComponent|printValues|format-list;gwmi -namespace root/MSCluster MSCluster_ClusterToNode -EA SilentlyContinue|select __CLASS,Antecedent,Dependent|printValues|format-list;gwmi -namespace root/virtualization/v2 Msvm_ComputerSystem -EA SilentlyContinue|select __CLASS,Name|printValues|format-list;gwmi -namespace root/virtualization Msvm_ComputerSystem -EA SilentlyContinue|select __CLASS,Name|printValues|format-list;gwmi -namespace root/MSCluster MSCluster_Resource -EA SilentlyContinue|select __CLASS,PrivateProperties,Name,Type|printValues|format-list;gwmi -namespace root\cimv2 Win32_ComputerSystem -EA SilentlyContinue|select __CLASS,Domain,Name|printValues|format-list;gwmi -namespace root\cimv2 Win32_OperatingSystem -EA SilentlyContinue|select __CLASS,Caption,Version|printValues|format-list;gwmi -namespace root/MSCluster MSCluster_Node -EA SilentlyContinue|select __CLASS,Name|printValues|format-list;'---SNC-SEPERATOR---REGISTRY-SECTION---';Get-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:/SYSTEM/CurrentControlSet/Services/Tcpip/Parameters' -EA SilentlyContinue|select PSPath,Domain,Hostname|format-list;}"

description: powershell is a scripting language and command-line shell that is bundled with all modern versions of windows. while this tool is used for all kinds of legitimate purposes, attackers also leverage it to compromise windows systems. additionally, attackers frequently use obfuscation techniques in an attempt to bypass detection mechanisms. however, if obfuscation is detected, it can be a telltale sign of malicious intent. this rule detects a powershell argument that has high number of dollar sign characters, indicating many variables were set. this is likely an unusual thing to see on the command line and could be an indicator of string obfuscation.

the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: powershell is a scripting language and command-line shell that is bundled with all modern versions of windows. while this tool is used for all kinds of legitimate purposes, attackers also leverage it to compromise windows systems. additionally, attackers frequently use obfuscation techniques in an attempt to bypass detection mechanisms. however, if obfuscation is detected, it can be a telltale sign of malicious intent. this rule detects a powershell argument that has high number of dollar sign characters, indicating many variables were set. this is likely an unusual thing to see on the command line and could be an indicator of string obfuscation.

- Thad","closed_state":"Unknown","confidence":"Medium","context":null,"description":"PowerShell is a scripting language and command-line shell that is bundled with all modern versions of Windows. While this tool is used for all kinds of legitimate purposes, attackers also leverage it to compromise Windows systems. Additionally, attackers frequently use obfuscation techniques in an attempt to bypass detection mechanisms. However, if obfuscation is detected, it can be a telltale sign of malicious intent. This rule detects a PowerShell argument that has high number of dollar sign characters, indicating many variables were set. description: this rule looks for a command with a very long argument. malware authors and windows attack tools often perform a variety of tasks using built-in commands. commands with a long list of arguments could indicate malicious activity. legitimate commands triggered by this rule should be tuned accordingly.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-21-2038235515-108931498-1364944312-435520   account name:  snowdisco   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x35f4734e    target subject:   security id:  s-1-0-0   account name:  -   account domain:  -   logon id:  0x0    process information:   new process id:  0x3590   new process name: c:\windows\system32\windowspowershell\v1.0\powershell.exe   token elevation type: %%1936   mandatory label:  s-1-16-12288   creator process id: 0x1220   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\cmd.exe   process command line: powershell  -noninteractive -windowstyle hidden -command "& {mode con lines=1 cols=32766; set-variable -name 'snc_iswmi' -value $true -scope global;  function printvalues{process{foreach-object{try{$a=$_.properties}catch{$a=$null}if(-not $a){$a=$_.psobject.properties}foreach($p in $a){$o=$p.name+' : ';if ($p.value -is [array]){$o+=[string]::join(',', $p.value);}else{$o+=$p.value;}$o;if($p.value -is [system.management.managementbaseobject] -or $p.value -is [system.management.managementbaseobject[]]){'_extended_start:';$p.value|printvalues;'_extended_end!';}}}}}gwmi -namespace root\cimv2 win32_process -ea silentlycontinue|select __class,caption,commandline,creationdate,description,executablepath,name,parentprocessid,processid,priority|printvalues|format-list;'---snc-seperator---registry-section---';}"     token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.
detail.args: powershell  -NonInteractive -WindowStyle Hidden -command "& {mode con lines=1 cols=32766; Set-Variable -Name 'SNC_isWmi' -Value $true -Scope Global;  function printValues{Process{foreach-object{try{$a=$_.Properties}catch{$a=$null}if(-not $a){$a=$_.PsObject.Properties}foreach($p in $a){$o=$p.Name+' : ';if ($p.Value -is [Array]){$o+=[string]::Join(',', $p.Value);}else{$o+=$p.Value;}$o;if($p.Value -is [System.Management.ManagementBaseObject] -or $p.Value -is [System.Management.ManagementBaseObject[]]){'_EXTENDED_START:';$p.Value|printValues;'_EXTENDED_END!';}}}}}gwmi -namespace root\cimv2 Win32_Process -EA SilentlyContinue|select __CLASS,Caption,CommandLine,CreationDate,Description,ExecutablePath,Name,ParentProcessId,ProcessId,Priority|printValues|format-list;'---SNC-SEPERATOR---REGISTRY-SECTION---';}"

description: powershell is a scripting language and command-line shell that is bundled with all modern versions of windows. while this tool is used for all kinds of legitimate purposes, attackers also leverage it to compromise windows systems. additionally, attackers frequently use obfuscation techniques in an attempt to bypass detection mechanisms. however, if obfuscation is detected, it can be a telltale sign of malicious intent. this rule detects a powershell argument that has high number of dollar sign characters, indicating many variables were set. this is likely an unusual thing to see on the command line and could be an indicator of string obfuscation.

pspath,versionstring,setupstring|format-list;}"     token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for a command with a very long argument. malware authors and windows attack tools often perform a variety of tasks using built-in commands. commands with a long list of arguments could indicate malicious activity. legitimate commands triggered by this rule should be tuned accordingly.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-21-2038235515-108931498-1364944312-435520   account name:  snowdisco   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x787943f9    target subject:   security id:  s-1-0-0   account name:  -   account domain:  -   logon id:  0x0    process information:   new process id:  0x10d8   new process name: c:\windows\system32\windowspowershell\v1.0\powershell.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-12288   creator process id: 0x64c   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\cmd.exe   process command line: powershell  -noninteractive -windowstyle hidden -command "& {mode con lines=1 cols=32766; set-variable -name 'snc_iswmi' -value $true -scope global;  function printvalues{process{foreach-object{try{$a=$_.properties}catch{$a=$null}if(-not $a){$a=$_.psobject.properties}foreach($p in $a){$o=$p.name+' : ';if ($p.value -is [array]){$o+=[string]::join(',', $p.value);}else{$o+=$p.value;}$o;if($p.value -is [system.management.managementbaseobject] -or $p.value -is [system.management.managementbaseobject[]]){'_extended_start:';$p.value|printvalues;'_extended_end!';}}}}}gwmi -namespace root\cimv2 win32_process -ea silentlycontinue|select __class,caption,commandline,creationdate,description,executablepath,name,parentprocessid,processid,priority|printvalues|format-list;'---snc-seperator---registry-section---';}"     token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.
detail.args: powershell  -NonInteractive -WindowStyle Hidden -command "& {mode con lines=1 cols=32766; Set-Variable -Name 'SNC_isWmi' -Value $true -Scope Global;  function printValues{Process{foreach-object{try{$a=$_.Properties}catch{$a=$null}if(-not $a){$a=$_.PsObject.Properties}foreach($p in $a){$o=$p.Name+' : ';if ($p.Value -is [Array]){$o+=[string]::Join(',', $p.Value);}else{$o+=$p.Value;}$o;if($p.Value -is [System.Management.ManagementBaseObject] -or $p.Value -is [System.Management.ManagementBaseObject[]]){'_EXTENDED_START:';$p.Value|printValues;'_EXTENDED_END!';}}}}}gwmi -namespace root\cimv2 Win32_Process -EA SilentlyContinue|select __CLASS,Caption,CommandLine,CreationDate,Description,ExecutablePath,Name,ParentProcessId,ProcessId,Priority|printValues|format-list;'---SNC-SEPERATOR---REGISTRY-SECTION---';}"

description: powershell is a scripting language and command-line shell that is bundled with all modern versions of windows. while this tool is used for all kinds of legitimate purposes, attackers also leverage it to compromise windows systems. additionally, attackers frequently use obfuscation techniques in an attempt to bypass detection mechanisms. however, if obfuscation is detected, it can be a telltale sign of malicious intent. this rule detects a powershell argument that has high number of dollar sign characters, indicating many variables were set. this is likely an unusual thing to see on the command line and could be an indicator of string obfuscation.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-21-2038235515-108931498-1364944312-435520   account name:  snowdisco   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x161d0bb8    target subject:   security id:  s-1-0-0   account name:  -   account domain:  -   logon id:  0x0    process information:   new process id:  0x60   new process name: c:\windows\system32\windowspowershell\v1.0\powershell.exe   token elevation type: %%1936   mandatory label:  s-1-16-12288   creator process id: 0x1d30   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\cmd.exe   process command line: powershell  -noninteractive -windowstyle hidden -command "& {mode con lines=1 cols=32766; set-variable -name 'snc_iswmi' -value $true -scope global;  function printvalues{process{foreach-object{try{$a=$_.properties}catch{$a=$null}if(-not $a){$a=$_.psobject.properties}foreach($p in $a){$o=$p.name+' : ';if ($p.value -is [array]){$o+=[string]::join(',', $p.value);}else{$o+=$p.value;}$o;if($p.value -is [system.management.managementbaseobject] -or $p.value -is [system.management.managementbaseobject[]]){'_extended_start:';$p.value|printvalues;'_extended_end!';}}}}}gwmi -namespace root\cimv2 win32_process -ea silentlycontinue|select __class,caption,commandline,creationdate,description,executablepath,name,parentprocessid,processid,priority|printvalues|format-list;'---snc-seperator---registry-section---';}"     token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.
detail.args: powershell  -NonInteractive -WindowStyle Hidden -command "& {mode con lines=1 cols=32766; Set-Variable -Name 'SNC_isWmi' -Value $true -Scope Global;  function printValues{Process{foreach-object{try{$a=$_.Properties}catch{$a=$null}if(-not $a){$a=$_.PsObject.Properties}foreach($p in $a){$o=$p.Name+' : ';if ($p.Value -is [Array]){$o+=[string]::Join(',', $p.Value);}else{$o+=$p.Value;}$o;if($p.Value -is [System.Management.ManagementBaseObject] -or $p.Value -is [System.Management.ManagementBaseObject[]]){'_EXTENDED_START:';$p.Value|printValues;'_EXTENDED_END!';}}}}}gwmi -namespace root\cimv2 Win32_Process -EA SilentlyContinue|select __CLASS,Caption,CommandLine,CreationDate,Description,ExecutablePath,Name,ParentProcessId,ProcessId,Priority|printValues|format-list;'---SNC-SEPERATOR---REGISTRY-SECTION---';}"

description: powershell is a scripting language and command-line shell that is bundled with all modern versions of windows. while this tool is used for all kinds of legitimate purposes, attackers also leverage it to compromise windows systems. additionally, attackers frequently use obfuscation techniques in an attempt to bypass detection mechanisms. however, if obfuscation is detected, it can be a telltale sign of malicious intent. this rule detects a powershell argument that has high number of dollar sign characters, indicating many variables were set. this is likely an unusual thing to see on the command line and could be an indicator of string obfuscation.

pspath,versionstring,setupstring|format-list;}"     token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: powershell is a scripting language and command-line shell that is bundled with all modern versions of windows. while this tool is used for all kinds of legitimate purposes, attackers also leverage it to compromise windows systems. additionally, attackers frequently use obfuscation techniques in an attempt to bypass detection mechanisms. however, if obfuscation is detected, it can be a telltale sign of malicious intent. this rule detects a powershell argument that has high number of dollar sign characters, indicating many variables were set. this is likely an unusual thing to see on the command line and could be an indicator of string obfuscation.

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  phrad06$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    target subject:   security id:  s-1-0-0   account name:  23447   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3de9ae2    process information:   new process id:  0x2b48   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-8192   creator process id: 0x30fc   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: "c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe" "c:\windows\system32\edgehtml.dll",#141 microsoft.windows.search_cw5n1h2txyewy    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for a rundll32 process execution with an ordinal argument. ordinal arguments can be used in rundll32 commands to invoke an exported function by position instead of by name. this technique has been used by various malware variants upon installation. if this rule triggers an alert, inspect the file executed by rundll32 and where it originated from. some legitimate applications may also use this method, so some tuning may be required.

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  prcp025$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    target subject:   security id:  s-1-5-21-2038235515-108931498-1364944312-30902   account name:  e102142   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x377b59    process information:   new process id:  0x36ac   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-8192   creator process id: 0x17e4   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: "c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe" "c:\windows\system32\edgehtml.dll",#141 microsoft.onedrivesync_8wekyb3d8bbwe    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for a rundll32 process execution with an ordinal argument. ordinal arguments can be used in rundll32 commands to invoke an exported function by position instead of by name. this technique has been used by various malware variants upon installation. if this rule triggers an alert, inspect the file executed by rundll32 and where it originated from. some legitimate applications may also use this method, so some tuning may be required.

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-19   account name:  local service   account domain:  nt authority   logon id:  0x3e5    target subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  prqm248$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    process information:   new process id:  0x5404   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-16384   creator process id: 0x2e90   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,davsetcookie rs http://rs/zenworksupdaterservice    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

description: this rule looks for a rundll32 process execution with an ordinal argument. ordinal arguments can be used in rundll32 commands to invoke an exported function by position instead of by name. this technique has been used by various malware variants upon installation. if this rule triggers an alert, inspect the file executed by rundll32 and where it originated from. some legitimate applications may also use this method, so some tuning may be required.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-19   account name:  local service   account domain:  nt authority   logon id:  0x3e5    target subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  dkor093$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    process information:   new process id:  0x5348   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-16384   creator process id: 0x24dc   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,davsetcookie rs http://rs/zenworksupdaterservice    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  prdr019$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    target subject:   security id:  s-1-0-0   account name:  35232   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0xf1a94    process information:   new process id:  0x2bb8   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-8192   creator process id: 0x23f0   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: "c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe" "c:\windows\system32\edgehtml.dll",#141 microsoft.windows.sechealthui_cw5n1h2txyewy    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for a rundll32 process execution with an ordinal argument. ordinal arguments can be used in rundll32 commands to invoke an exported function by position instead of by name. this technique has been used by various malware variants upon installation. if this rule triggers an alert, inspect the file executed by rundll32 and where it originated from. some legitimate applications may also use this method, so some tuning may be required.

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

--variations-seed-version --mojo-platform-channel-handle=5216 /pfhostedapp:0e42d6fbda70f98b263001e25f7dded7b3a1e51f /prefetch:1    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

closed_reason: the purpose of msedgewebview2.exe is to allow app developers to embed web content in windows applications. msedgewebview2.exe is the executable file for microsoft edge webview2, a web browser control that uses microsoft edge as a rendering engine
description: this rule looks for a command with a very long argument. malware authors and windows attack tools often perform a variety of tasks using built-in commands. commands with a long list of arguments could indicate malicious activity. legitimate commands triggered by this rule should be tuned accordingly.

--variations-seed-version --mojo-platform-channel-handle=5476 /pfhostedapp:f39eae91d875ef234ca0093532329fc3ffd30176 /prefetch:1    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

closed_reason: the purpose of msedgewebview2.exe is to allow app developers to embed web content in windows applications. msedgewebview2.exe is the executable file for microsoft edge webview2, a web browser control that uses microsoft edge as a rendering engine
description: this rule looks for a command with a very long argument. malware authors and windows attack tools often perform a variety of tasks using built-in commands. commands with a long list of arguments could indicate malicious activity. legitimate commands triggered by this rule should be tuned accordingly.

--variations-seed-version --mojo-platform-channel-handle=2364 /prefetch:14 /pfhostedapp:bc2b8602ddf3a1a4bc387a90677cb49125070e09    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

closed_reason: the purpose of msedgewebview2.exe is to allow app developers to embed web content in windows applications. msedgewebview2.exe is the executable file for microsoft edge webview2, a web browser control that uses microsoft edge as a rendering engine
description: this rule looks for a command with a very long argument. malware authors and windows attack tools often perform a variety of tasks using built-in commands. commands with a long list of arguments could indicate malicious activity. legitimate commands triggered by this rule should be tuned accordingly.

--variations-seed-version --mojo-platform-channel-handle=2364 \/prefetch:14 \/pfhostedapp:bc2b8602ddf3a1a4bc387a90677cb49125070e09    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as --variations-seed-version --mojo-platform-channel-handle=4516 /prefetch:10 /pfhostedapp:44ddd78ef04bfbe542b8771b8beec5dfed50776e    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

closed_reason: the purpose of msedgewebview2.exe is to allow app developers to embed web content in windows applications. msedgewebview2.exe is the executable file for microsoft edge webview2, a web browser control that uses microsoft edge as a rendering engine
description: this rule looks for a command with a very long argument. malware authors and windows attack tools often perform a variety of tasks using built-in commands. commands with a long list of arguments could indicate malicious activity. legitimate commands triggered by this rule should be tuned accordingly.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-19   account name:  local service   account domain:  nt authority   logon id:  0x3e5    target subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  gldo019$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    process information:   new process id:  0xa74   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-16384   creator process id: 0x281c   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,davsetcookie fien1238 http://fien1238/zvqfuner/ybpnyynhapu/zvqnf/fien1238.cnexivrj.pbz/znn/nccfgneg/pnerztzg/nccfgneg.rkr    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

description: this rule looks for a rundll32 process execution with an ordinal argument. ordinal arguments can be used in rundll32 commands to invoke an exported function by position instead of by name. this technique has been used by various malware variants upon installation. if this rule triggers an alert, inspect the file executed by rundll32 and where it originated from. some legitimate applications may also use this method, so some tuning may be required.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-19   account name:  local service   account domain:  nt authority   logon id:  0x3e5    target subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  btis657$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    process information:   new process id:  0x6b0   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-16384   creator process id: 0x5438   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,davsetcookie rs http://rs/zenworksupdaterservice    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

description: this rule looks for a rundll32 process execution with an ordinal argument. ordinal arguments can be used in rundll32 commands to invoke an exported function by position instead of by name. this technique has been used by various malware variants upon installation. if this rule triggers an alert, inspect the file executed by rundll32 and where it originated from. some legitimate applications may also use this method, so some tuning may be required.

Some legitimate applications may also use this method, so some tuning may be required.","distinguisher_key":"prrd098.parkview.com","distinguishers":{"hostname":"prrd098.parkview.com"},"events_count":2,"events_threshold":1,"external_id":"","first_event_at":"2025-07-23T13:07:25.349000Z","last_event_at":"2025-07-23T13:08:24.004000Z","external_ips":[],"external_ips_count":0,"info_links":["https:\/\/unit42.paloaltonetworks.com\/unit42-the-dukes-rd-finds-a-new-anti-analysis-technique\/","https:\/\/attack.mitre.org\/techniques\/T1218\/011\/"],"internal_ips":[],"internal_ips_count":0,"is_suppressed":false,"is_threat":false,"is_tuned":false,"kill_chain":["5 - Installation"],"message":"WINDOWS METHODOLOGY [Rundll32 Ordinal Arg]","mitre_ids":["T1587.001","T1588.001","T0894","T1218.011","T1085","T1218"],"metaclasses":{"windows":2},"mongo_id":"6880dec5223863104518e9e8","origin_id":"map_rule","products":{},"risk":"Low","risk_order":1,"search":"((metaclass:windows ((class=ms_defender category:`advancedhunting-deviceprocessevents`) OR (source:`microsoft-windows-sysmon` eventid=1) OR (source:`microsoft-windows-security-auditing` eventid=4688))) OR (class:fireeye_hx_ioc assets.properties.os:\/windows\/ eventtype:processevent)) [process,processpath]:`\\rundll32.exe` args:\/#\\d+\/ NOT srcipv4:$exclusions.global.srcipv4","seconds_threshold":60,"severity":"Low","source_revision":0,"state":"Closed","tags":["windows","methodology","malware","Defense Evasion","System Binary Proxy Execution","T1218","Rundll32","T1218.011","md-info"],"threat_changed_at":null,"threat_type":5,"trigger_id":"1.1.2305","trigger_revision":36,"tuning_search":"","risk_score":20,"asset_ids":[16435608,77530516],"threat_vector":["endpoint"],"meta_ts":"2025-07-23T13:08:32.153101Z","class":"alerts","__metadata__":{"id":"1982364","customer_id":"hexsnp679","timestamp":"2025-07-23T13:08:32.153101Z"}}

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-19   account name:  local service   account domain:  nt authority   logon id:  0x3e5    target subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  br1rd16$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    process information:   new process id:  0x5c78   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-16384   creator process id: 0x1298   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,davsetcookie fien1238 http://fien1238/zvqfuner/ybpnyynhapu/zvqnf/fien1238.cnexivrj.pbz/znn/nccfgneg/pnerztzg/nccfgneg.rkr    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-19   account name:  local service   account domain:  nt authority   logon id:  0x3e5    target subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  btis007$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    process information:   new process id:  0x37ac   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-16384   creator process id: 0x4110   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,davsetcookie rs http://rs/zenworksupdaterservice    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-19   account name:  local service   account domain:  nt authority   logon id:  0x3e5    target subject:   security id:  s-1-5-21-2038235515-108931498-1364944312-454915   account name:  a405793   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x1456a0    process information:   new process id:  0x1d48   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-8192   creator process id: 0x17dc   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,davsetcookie parkviewhealth-my.sharepoint.com@ssl https://parkviewhealth-my.sharepoint.com/personal/sierra_mcdowell_parkview_com/documents    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-21-2038235515-108931498-1364944312-333175   account name:  e109792   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x8df06b6e    target subject:   security id:  s-1-0-0   account name:  -   account domain:  -   logon id:  0x0    process information:   new process id:  0x44bc   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-8192   creator process id: 0xa40   creator process name: c:\program files (x86)\microsoft\edge\application\msedge.exe   process command line: "c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe" "c:\windows\system32\dfshim.dll",shopenverbapplication http://svra2582.parkview.com/smartclient/adminclient.application    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for suspicious rundll32.exe process executions with command lines that invoke shopenverbapplication or shopenverbshortcut functions from dfshim.dll. attackers leverage this technique to execute clickonce applications (.application or .appref-ms files) from remote urls. this often serves as a method to bypass application whitelisting (awl) and achieve arbitrary code execution.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-19   account name:  local service   account domain:  nt authority   logon id:  0x3e5    target subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  hcad001$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    process information:   new process id:  0x5f90   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-16384   creator process id: 0x5bf0   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,davsetcookie rs http://rs/zenworksupdaterservice    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

description: this rule looks for a rundll32 process execution with an ordinal argument. ordinal arguments can be used in rundll32 commands to invoke an exported function by position instead of by name. this technique has been used by various malware variants upon installation. if this rule triggers an alert, inspect the file executed by rundll32 and where it originated from. some legitimate applications may also use this method, so some tuning may be required.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  prrad203$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    target subject:   security id:  s-1-0-0   account name:  x006539   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0xfeb0f41    process information:   new process id:  0x245c   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-8192   creator process id: 0x2744   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: "c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe" "c:\windows\system32\edgehtml.dll",#141 microsoft.windows.search_cw5n1h2txyewy    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for a rundll32 process execution with an ordinal argument. ordinal arguments can be used in rundll32 commands to invoke an exported function by position instead of by name. this technique has been used by various malware variants upon installation. if this rule triggers an alert, inspect the file executed by rundll32 and where it originated from. some legitimate applications may also use this method, so some tuning may be required.
