description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-19   account name:  local service   account domain:  nt authority   logon id:  0x3e5    target subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  ph1bh001$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    process information:   new process id:  0xf54   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-16384   creator process id: 0x2ad4   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,davsetcookie rs http://rs/zenworksupdaterservice    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

description: this rule looks for a rundll32 process execution with an ordinal argument. ordinal arguments can be used in rundll32 commands to invoke an exported function by position instead of by name. this technique has been used by various malware variants upon installation. if this rule triggers an alert, inspect the file executed by rundll32 and where it originated from. some legitimate applications may also use this method, so some tuning may be required.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-19   account name:  local service   account domain:  nt authority   logon id:  0x3e5    target subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  prqm053$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    process information:   new process id:  0x80ac   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-16384   creator process id: 0x2028   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,davsetcookie rs http://rs/zenworksupdaterservice    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-19   account name:  local service   account domain:  nt authority   logon id:  0x3e5    target subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  mpts006$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    process information:   new process id:  0x4e94   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-16384   creator process id: 0x143c   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,davsetcookie fie01352 http://fie01352/genhznonfrncc%24/bv9/bvafvtug.rkr    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

vcontainer

vidia corporation\shadowplay
vspapi64.dll" #14 7400 -org=unknown    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for a rundll32 process execution with an ordinal argument. ordinal arguments can be used in rundll32 commands to invoke an exported function by position instead of by name. this technique has been used by various malware variants upon installation. if this rule triggers an alert, inspect the file executed by rundll32 and where it originated from. some legitimate applications may also use this method, so some tuning may be required.

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-21-2038235515-108931498-1364944312-187356   account name:  e143493   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3941fd    target subject:   security id:  s-1-0-0   account name:  -   account domain:  -   logon id:  0x0    process information:   new process id:  0x36e4   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-8192   creator process id: 0x3168   creator process name: c:\program files (x86)\microsoft\edge\application\msedge.exe   process command line: "c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe" "c:\windows\system32\dfshim.dll",shopenverbapplication http://svra0903.parkview.com/smartclient/adminclient.application    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for suspicious rundll32.exe process executions with command lines that invoke shopenverbapplication or shopenverbshortcut functions from dfshim.dll. attackers leverage this technique to execute clickonce applications (.application or .appref-ms files) from remote urls. this often serves as a method to bypass application whitelisting (awl) and achieve arbitrary code execution.

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

closed_reason: msedge.exe is the file name for microsoft edge, a web browser that replaced internet explorer in 2020. it's based on google's chromium browser technology.
description: this rule looks for a command with a very long argument. malware authors and windows attack tools often perform a variety of tasks using built-in commands. commands with a long list of arguments could indicate malicious activity. legitimate commands triggered by this rule should be tuned accordingly.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-21-2038235515-108931498-1364944312-40913   account name:  x006539   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0xfeb0f41    target subject:   security id:  s-1-0-0   account name:  -   account domain:  -   logon id:  0x0    process information:   new process id:  0x3a54   new process name: c:\program files (x86)\microsoft\edge\application\msedge.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-8192   creator process id: 0x370c   creator process name: c:\program files (x86)\microsoft\edge\application\138.0.3351.95\notification_helper.exe   process command line: "c:\program files (x86)\microsoft\edge\application\msedge.exe" --winrt-background-task-    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

closed_reason: msedge.exe is the file name for microsoft edge, a web browser that replaced internet explorer in 2020. it's based on google's chromium browser technology.
description: this rule looks for a command with a long string of consecutive base64 characters. this rule merely matches on characters and does not attempt to decode, so it could trigger on a string that is not base64-encoded. malware authors and windows attack tools will often encode long strings of commands in base64, with powershell directly supporting encoded commands. this could indicate malicious activity.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-19   account name:  local service   account domain:  nt authority   logon id:  0x3e5    target subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  lrfm121$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    process information:   new process id:  0x52dc   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-16384   creator process id: 0x4110   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,davsetcookie rs http://rs/zenworksupdaterservice    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  ohrx010$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    target subject:   security id:  s-1-0-0   account name:  e151281   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x109ba8b    process information:   new process id:  0x150c   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-8192   creator process id: 0x22b0   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: "c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe" "c:\windows\system32\edgehtml.dll",#141 microsoft.windows.sechealthui_cw5n1h2txyewy    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for a rundll32 process execution with an ordinal argument. ordinal arguments can be used in rundll32 commands to invoke an exported function by position instead of by name. this technique has been used by various malware variants upon installation. if this rule triggers an alert, inspect the file executed by rundll32 and where it originated from. some legitimate applications may also use this method, so some tuning may be required.
search: ((metaclass:windows ((class=ms_defender category:`advancedhunting-deviceprocessevents`) or (source:`microsoft-windows-sysmon` eventid=1) or (source:`microsoft-windows-security-auditing` eventid=4688))) or (class:fireeye_hx_ioc assets.properties.os:/windows/ eventtype:processevent)) [process,processpath]:`\rundll32.exe` args:/#\d+/ not srcipv4:$exclusions.global.srcipv4

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-19   account name:  local service   account domain:  nt authority   logon id:  0x3e5    target subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  btis288$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    process information:   new process id:  0x16ed4   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-16384   creator process id: 0x776c   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,davsetcookie rs http://rs/zenworksupdaterservice    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-19   account name:  local service   account domain:  nt authority   logon id:  0x3e5    target subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  prrd036$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    process information:   new process id:  0x1fe8   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-16384   creator process id: 0x83c   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,davsetcookie rs http://rs/zenworksupdaterservice    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.

description: this rule looks for a rundll32 process execution with an ordinal argument. ordinal arguments can be used in rundll32 commands to invoke an exported function by position instead of by name. this technique has been used by various malware variants upon installation. if this rule triggers an alert, inspect the file executed by rundll32 and where it originated from. some legitimate applications may also use this method, so some tuning may be required.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  ec2tr217$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    target subject:   security id:  s-1-0-0   account name:  epicsim1   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x377395a    process information:   new process id:  0x21a4   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-8192   creator process id: 0x13f0   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: "c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe" "c:\windows\system32\edgehtml.dll",#141 microsoft.windows.sechealthui_cw5n1h2txyewy    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for a rundll32 process execution with an ordinal argument. ordinal arguments can be used in rundll32 commands to invoke an exported function by position instead of by name. this technique has been used by various malware variants upon installation. if this rule triggers an alert, inspect the file executed by rundll32 and where it originated from. some legitimate applications may also use this method, so some tuning may be required.

closed_reason: the purpose of msedgewebview2.exe is to allow app developers to embed web content in windows applications. msedgewebview2.exe is the executable file for microsoft edge webview2, a web browser control that uses microsoft edge as a rendering engine
description: this rule looks for a command with a very long argument. malware authors and windows attack tools often perform a variety of tasks using built-in commands. commands with a long list of arguments could indicate malicious activity. legitimate commands triggered by this rule should be tuned accordingly.

--variations-seed-version --mojo-platform-channel-handle=5076 \/pfhostedapp:4d3e9fb677fb3ccccf8bf3eb40860f39108522d3 \/prefetch:1    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as --variations-seed-version --mojo-platform-channel-handle=4872 /prefetch:14 /pfhostedapp:75ab266375df9b2ca8064514c7f854f50a5d998e    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

closed_reason: the purpose of msedgewebview2.exe is to allow app developers to embed web content in windows applications. msedgewebview2.exe is the executable file for microsoft edge webview2, a web browser control that uses microsoft edge as a rendering engine
description: this rule looks for a command with a very long argument. malware authors and windows attack tools often perform a variety of tasks using built-in commands. commands with a long list of arguments could indicate malicious activity. legitimate commands triggered by this rule should be tuned accordingly.

--variations-seed-version --mojo-platform-channel-handle=4756 /prefetch:14 /pfhostedapp:482745135ec8ce1527418149018a3ebd310158ba    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

closed_reason: the purpose of msedgewebview2.exe is to allow app developers to embed web content in windows applications. msedgewebview2.exe is the executable file for microsoft edge webview2, a web browser control that uses microsoft edge as a rendering engine
description: this rule looks for a command with a very long argument. malware authors and windows attack tools often perform a variety of tasks using built-in commands. commands with a long list of arguments could indicate malicious activity. legitimate commands triggered by this rule should be tuned accordingly.

--variations-seed-version --mojo-platform-channel-handle=5096 /prefetch:14 /pfhostedapp:35cdd06d810ef0b79ae4230928785ea1c7836825    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

closed_reason: the purpose of msedgewebview2.exe is to allow app developers to embed web content in windows applications. msedgewebview2.exe is the executable file for microsoft edge webview2, a web browser control that uses microsoft edge as a rendering engine
description: this rule looks for a command with a very long argument. malware authors and windows attack tools often perform a variety of tasks using built-in commands. commands with a long list of arguments could indicate malicious activity. legitimate commands triggered by this rule should be tuned accordingly.

detail.msg: a new process has been created.    creator subject:   security id:  s-1-5-19   account name:  local service   account domain:  nt authority   logon id:  0x3e5    target subject:   security id:  s-1-5-18   account name:  btrx001$   account domain:  phs   logon id:  0x3e7    process information:   new process id:  0x2f90   new process name: c:\windows\system32\rundll32.exe   token elevation type: tokenelevationtypedefault (1)   mandatory label:  s-1-16-16384   creator process id: 0x63f0   creator process name: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe   process command line: rundll32.exe c:\windows\system32\davclnt.dll,davsetcookie svra0019 http://svra0019/installsource/adobe/acrobat/acrobat%20dc%2021.0    token elevation type indicates the type of token that was assigned to the new process in accordance with user account control policy.    type 1 is a full token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  a full token is only used if user account control is disabled or if the user is the built-in administrator account or a service account.    type 2 is an elevated token with no privileges removed or groups disabled.  an elevated token is used when user account control is enabled and the user chooses to start the program using run as administrator.  an elevated token is also used when an application is configured to always require administrative privilege or to always require maximum privilege, and the user is a member of the administrators group.    type 3 is a limited token with administrative privileges removed and administrative groups disabled.  the limited token is used when user account control is enabled, the application does not require administrative privilege, and the user does not choose to start the program using run as administrator.

description: this rule looks for command line arguments related to rundll32.exe being used to execute a webdav client to access a resource. while this can be legitimate activity, this technique has also been used by malicious actors and malware to disguise the retrieval and execution of remote payloads. identify and assess the remote source that was accessed to determine if this is legitimate activity.
